$schema_note: Catalog of standard material indices from Ashby, Materials Selection in Mechanical Design (6th ed.), Appendix C. 'maximize' means materials with the highest value of the expression are best for that function. expr uses these tokens: E (Young's modulus, GPa), G (shear modulus, GPa), sigma_f (failure strength, MPa), rho (density, kg/m^3), Cm (price USD/kg), Cp (specific heat J/kg/K), lambda (thermal conductivity W/m/K), alpha (thermal expansion 1/K), rho_e (electrical resistivity), eta (loss coefficient), K1c (fracture toughness MPa*m^0.5), nu (Poisson's ratio). All indices listed assume the index is to be MAXIMIZED unless stated otherwise.

Elastic hinges: minimum radius of bend (max flexibility without failure)
Knife edges, pivots: minimum contact area, maximum bearing load
Diaphragms: maximum deflection under specified pressure or force

Thermal insulation, minimize energy in thermal cycle

Bus bars: minimum life cost, high current conductor
Electromagnet windings, short pulse: no mechanical failure
High-speed electric motor windings: max rotational speed
Relay arms: minimum response time, no fatigue failure
